# Aura Teaching Context

## Curriculum Summary
- Subject: Physics
- Grade level: 9
- Topics: momentum
- Learning goals: Understand momentum; Apply momentum with examples

## Core Concept List
- Quantity of motion and mass
- Changing motion quickly
- Molecular momentum
- Car momentum concept
- Momentum scale difference
- Linear momentum formula
- Object motion has momentum
- Team momentum builds and continues
- Momentum unit standard
- Momentum direction equals velocity
- Large momentum condition
- Momentum of rest
- Momentum equation formula
- Momentum relationship with mass and velocity
- Momentum guides problem thinking
- Relative speed comparison
- Object motion quantity
- Player momentum comparison
- Tight end speed advantage

- 18. Tight end speed advantage (diagram)
- 19. Player momentum comparison (diagram)

## Common Misconceptions
- Momentum of rest: Objects at rest do not have momentum.

## Useful Analogies
- Quantity of motion and mass: Unlike kinetic energy, momentum depends equally on an object’s mass and velocity.
- Momentum scale difference: The momentum of a molecule and a car are vastly different, differing by 27 orders of magnitude (a factor of a billion billion billion).
- Team momentum builds and continues: In sports, a team that has momentum is on the move and is going to take some effort to stop.
- Momentum guides problem thinking: The momentum equation serves as a 'guide to thinking' and not merely a 'plug-and-chug recipe for algebraic problem-solving.'

## Prerequisites
Use graph relations marked `requires` and nearby concept cards.

## Accessibility Notes
- Disability profile: adhd, dyslexia
- Pace: slow
- Preferred modality: mixed
- Keep text short, use concrete examples, and avoid visible scoring language.
